codeReview.summary: The implementation correctly adds the Local Tokens management UI to the settings panel. renderLocalTokens() displays each saved token with symbol, full 0x address, chain badge, and a remove button. removeTokenFromLocalList() properly filters by address+chainId, updates localStorage, re-renders the list, and refreshes autocomplete. The empty state shows 'No custom tokens saved'. Rendering is triggered on settings modal open, after token save via popup, and on page load. All user-controlled strings (address, symbol, chainName) are properly escaped with escapeHtml(). The implementation follows the project's brutalist styling and never-truncate-addresses convention.

issues.description: token.chainId is interpolated directly into the data-chain-id HTML attribute without escapeHtml(). Same on line 2058 for the remove button. Since localStorage is user-controllable, a crafted chainId value (e.g. '" onclick="alert(1)') could theoretically break out of the attribute. Self-XSS only (user would have to manually edit their own localStorage), but inconsistent with the escaping applied to address and symbol. Should use escapeHtml(token.chainId) for consistency.

In the chainName fallback ('Chain ' + token.chainId), if chainId is crafted to contain HTML, the result would be passed through escapeHtml() on line 2057, so this is safe. However, the unescaped chainId in data attributes on lines 2054/2058 remains the concern.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker did not add automated tests for the new UI functions (renderLocalTokens, removeTokenFromLocalList). The skill procedure says 'For client-side-only changes: Testing is done via manual verification since the UI is inline HTML'. This is documented and justified, but the growing amount of client-side logic (localStorage CRUD, DOM rendering, event delegation) suggests the project may benefit from extracting testable logic into separate modules or adding integration tests.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff tests.added is empty. Skill SKILL.md step 2 explicitly exempts client-side-only changes from test requirement.

sharedStateObservations.observation: When building HTML strings with user data from localStorage, escapeHtml() is applied inconsistently — used for address and symbol but not for chainId in data attributes. The AGENTS.md convention about localStorage says 'always wrap reads in try/catch, handle corrupt JSON gracefully' (which is done), but doesn't mention HTML-escaping all interpolated values from localStorage.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/server.ts line 2054: data-chain-id uses raw token.chainId; compare to data-address which uses escapeHtml(token.address)

summary: Feature passes review. The Local Tokens management UI is correctly implemented with proper settings panel integration, remove functionality, empty state, and multi-point rendering triggers. All expected behaviors from the feature description are covered. Two non-blocking issues found: token.chainId is not HTML-escaped in data attributes (self-XSS only, low risk), and no automated tests were added (justified by architecture). The worker followed the skill procedure and performed thorough interactive verification.
